mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not above the unconditional baseline after FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year period, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income prospects, and lower expected corporate earnings, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not significantly below the unconditional mean at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A joint slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST identifies weakening final demand and housing-led consumption spillovers, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in the lower signal quartile across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO relative to PAYEMS indicates improving output per worker and easing unit-cost pressure, which supports margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction between INDPRO and PAYEMS is not significant out of sample, or if periods with rising INDPRO and flat-to-weak PAYEMS fail to generate above-baseline 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and inflation risk pricing rises, which compresses equity multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-over-policy spread does not predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or if triggered episodes have a positive mean forward return over any sustained sample of at least 50 events.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after prior weakness signals improving aggregate demand and earnings expectations, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 momentum does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is not distinguishable from noise in at least one complete business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE marks a true labor-market downturn, which weakens consumption and earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor downturn trigger does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered samples do not show a statistically negative average 20-day SPX return.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led slowdown, falling HOUST indicates tighter credit transmission and weaker durable-goods demand, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing declines do not precede below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the trigger fails to produce a negative average return across multiple rolling out-of-sample periods.
